Test Case Title: Export - Structural: Export Count Matches Displayed Records
Preconditions:
1. User is logged in and authenticated
2. User is on the Filter Properties page
Test Steps:
1. Set Energy Rating filter to A
2. Set Council filter to 'LONDON BOROUGH OF BEXLEY'
3. View Properties Page and wait for View Properties page and table to load
4. Capture the displayed properties count from the results count field
5. Export filtered data
6. Capture the exported record count with the displayed count
Expected Results:
1. Energy Rating filter is set to A
2. Council filter is set to 'LONDON BOROUGH OF BEXLEY'
3. View Properties page loads with filtered results and table content visible
4. Displayed count is greater than 0
5. Export completes successfully returning a list of records
6. Exported record count equals the displayed count exactly

Test Case Title: Export - Structural: Verify Export Column Order Matches Specification
Preconditions:
1. User is logged in and authenticated
2. A CSV export has been downloaded from the View Properties page with any filters applied
3. The 'CSV Export Specification & Alignment.xlsx' file attached to the story is available for reference
Test Steps:
1. Open the 'CSV Export Specification & Alignment.xlsx' file and note the defined column order
2. Open the exported CSV and compare each column's left-to-right position against the specification order
Expected Results:
1. Column order is identified from the specification
2. Every column appears in exactly the same left-to-right position as defined in the specification with no columns out of position
Comments: Column name correctness is verified separately by TC-2048.

Test Case Title: Export - Structural: Verify Export Column Names Match Specification
Preconditions:
1. User is logged in and authenticated
2. A CSV export has been downloaded from the View Properties page with any filters applied
3. The 'CSV Export Specification & Alignment.xlsx' file attached to the story is available for reference
Test Steps:
1. Open the exported CSV
2. Compare each column header name in the first row against the specification ? verify no columns are missing and no extra columns are present
Expected Results:
1. Every column name in the export matches the specification exactly
2. No columns defined in the specification are missing from the export
3. No extra columns are present in the export that are not defined in the specification

Test Case Title: Export - Structural: Fields without values show 'Not found' in export
Preconditions:
1. User is logged in and authenticated
2. User is on the Filter Properties page
3. The export contains at least one property where one or more placeholder-based fields are not populated from source data
Test Steps:
1. Apply filters and export the CSV
2. Locate rows where source data for placeholder-based fields is missing (for example, a row where both PossibleEvidenceEpcTransactionType and PossibleEvidenceSiccode are false; or a property with no EPC data)
3. Verify fields that use the placeholder convention display 'Not found' instead of blank ? including EPC energy rating, EPC expiry date and EPC certificate link for properties with no EPC data
Expected Results:
1. Export completes successfully with at least one record
2. At least one row with missing source data for placeholder-based fields is found
3. Each placeholder-based field under those conditions displays 'Not found' exactly (case-sensitive), not blank or any alternative text ? including EPC energy rating, EPC expiry date and EPC certificate link for properties with no EPC data

Test Case Title: Export - Structural: Exported directly-mapped field values match DMS API
Preconditions:
1. User is logged in and authenticated
2. A CSV export has been downloaded from the View Properties page with Energy Rating 'A' and Council 'LONDON BOROUGH OF BEXLEY' filters applied
3. DMS API is accessible for cross-referencing field values (DMS_BASE_URL and EXPORT_KEY configured)
Test Steps:
1. Apply Energy Rating 'A' and Council 'LONDON BOROUGH OF BEXLEY' filters and export the CSV
2. Fetch a reference property from the DMS API and locate it in the export by UPRN
3. Compare the following directly-mapped fields between the export and DMS API: Property address, UPRN, Property type, Rateable value (GBP), Property owner 1 name, Property owner 1 location, Property owner 1 address, Property owner 1 SIC code(s), EPC energy rating band, EPC energy rating, EPC expiry date, EPC (rating; expiry; transaction type), EPC certificate link, EPC transaction type
4. Confirm that Possible rental evidence from EPC register, Possible rental evidence from Companies House, PRS exemption status, PRS exemption date and Comments are not compared in this test as they are either computed from DMS booleans or sourced from Salesforce rather than DMS
Expected Results:
1. Export completes successfully with at least one record
2. A matching DMS property is found in the export by UPRN
3. All directly-mapped field values match the DMS API source exactly
4. Computed and Salesforce-dependent fields are excluded from comparison and covered by dedicated tests

Test Case Title: Export - Structural: Exported PRS Exemption Status and Comments match Salesforce data
Preconditions:
1. User is logged in and authenticated
2. User is on the Filter Properties page
3. The filtered results (Energy Rating 'A', Council 'LONDON BOROUGH OF BEXLEY', Postcode 'DA1 4AL') contain exactly 1 property with a non-empty PRS exemption status
4. The Property Details page is accessible
Test Steps:
1. Apply Energy Rating 'A', Council 'LONDON BOROUGH OF BEXLEY' and Postcode 'DA1 4AL' filters on the Filter Properties page and navigate to View Properties
2. Verify the results count shows exactly 1 result confirming only the known reference property is returned
3. Export the filtered CSV
4. Click 'View details' for the property to open the Property Details page; select the 'Property details' tab and record the UPRN
5. Locate the reference property in the export by UPRN
6. Select the 'PRS exemptions and penalties' tab on the Property Details page
7. Compare 'PRS exemption status' in the export against the value displayed on the 'PRS exemptions and penalties' tab
8. Extract comment bodies from the export 'Comments' field (entries are 'body by author on date' separated by ' | ') and compare against the comment bodies from the Property Details page (attribution lines starting with 'Added by' are excluded)
Expected Results:
1. Filters are applied and View Properties page loads with exactly 1 matching record
2. Results count shows exactly '1 results' confirming a single known property is returned
3. Export completes successfully and returns 1 record
4. Property Details page opens successfully and UPRN is recorded from the 'Property details' tab
5. The reference property is found in the export by UPRN
6. 'PRS exemptions and penalties' tab is displayed and active
7. 'PRS exemption status' in the export matches the Property Details page value exactly
8. All comment bodies from the Property Details page appear in the export 'Comments' field in the same order separated by ' | '

Test Case Title: Export - Structural: Verify Exported Field Formats and Empty State Values
Preconditions:
1. User is logged in and authenticated
2. A CSV export has been downloaded from the View Properties page
Test Steps:
1. Inspect date fields (EPC expiry date, PRS exemption date) across all rows - check both populated and unpopulated values
2. Inspect the 'PRS exemption status' field for a row where the property has no PRS exemption
Expected Results:
1. Populated date fields display in YYYY-MM-DD format; unpopulated EPC expiry date shows 'Not found'; unpopulated PRS exemption date is empty
2. PRS exemption status shows 'Data not found' when the property has no PRS exemption
Comments: PRS exemption date in the export is currently formatted as DD/MM/YYYY instead of YYYY-MM-DD (confirmed by TC-2003 normalisation comment) ? no bug raised yet. SIC code format checks removed from this test case: owner fields being empty when no owner data is covered by the 'Export - Field values when source data is absent' tests; SIC code format with owner data (pipe-separated values and N/A) is covered by dedicated TC-2016.

Test Case Title: Export - Field Values: Property owner column sets in export equal the maximum landlord count
Preconditions:
1. User is logged in and authenticated
2. A CSV export has been downloaded from the View Properties page with filters applied
Test Steps:
1. Apply filters and export the CSV
2. Identify the highest property owner index N present in the export column headers (e.g. if 'Property owner 3 name' exists but 'Property owner 4 name' does not then N = 3)
3. Using the DMS Export API fetch the same filtered data and find the property with the highest number of landlords and record that count
4. Compare N against the maximum landlord count from DMS
Expected Results:
1. Export completes successfully with at least one record
2. The maximum owner index N is identified from the CSV column headers
3. The property with the most landlords is identified in the DMS data and its landlord count is recorded
4. N equals the maximum landlord count ? all four column groups (name, location, address, SIC code(s)) are present for every owner index from 1 to N with no gaps or extra groups

Test Case Title: Export - Structural: Exported CSV filename follows the MEES_Properties_YYYY-MM-DDThh-mm-ss.csv pattern
Preconditions:
1. User is logged in and authenticated
2. User is on the Filter Properties page
Test Steps:
1. Apply filters and trigger the CSV export from the View Properties page
2. Capture the filename of the downloaded CSV file
Expected Results:
1. Export triggers successfully and a CSV file is downloaded
2. The filename matches the pattern MEES_Properties_YYYY-MM-DDThh-mm-ss.csv ? where YYYY is a 4-digit year; MM is a 2-digit month; DD is a 2-digit day; T is a literal separator; hh is a 2-digit hour; mm is a 2-digit minute; ss is a 2-digit second (for example MEES_Properties_2026-04-28T13-38-21.csv)

Test Case Title: SIC code fields are always present in every landlord object
Preconditions:
1. DMS API is accessible with a valid function key
2. UPRN 100022917842 returns a response with at least one landlord
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /mees/property?uprn=100022917842 with a valid x-functions-key header
2. Parse the JSON response body and retrieve the landlords array
3. For each landlord object in the array inspect the object keys
Expected Results:
1. Response returns HTTP 200 and valid JSON
2. The landlords array contains at least one entry
3. Every landlord object contains all four keys: sicCodeSicText1, sicCodeSicText2, sicCodeSicText3 and sicCodeSicText4

Test Case Title: SIC code fields are populated sequentially with no gaps
Preconditions:
1. DMS API is accessible with a valid function key
2. UPRN 100022917842 returns a response with at least one landlord
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /mees/property?uprn=100022917842 with a valid x-functions-key header
2. Parse the JSON response body and retrieve the landlords array
3. For each landlord in the array: if sicCodeSicText2 is non-null assert sicCodeSicText1 is also non-null; if sicCodeSicText3 is non-null assert sicCodeSicText2 is also non-null; if sicCodeSicText4 is non-null assert sicCodeSicText3 is also non-null
Expected Results:
1. Response returns HTTP 200 and valid JSON
2. The landlords array contains at least one entry
3. No landlord object has a null SIC code field followed by a non-null SIC code field ? SIC codes are populated from position 1 sequentially with no gaps

Test Case Title: Offshore landlord returns all SIC code fields as null
Preconditions:
1. DMS API is accessible with a valid function key
2. UPRN 10010248290 returns a response with both Onshore and Offshore landlords
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /mees/property?uprn=10010248290 with a valid x-functions-key header
2. Parse the JSON response body and retrieve the landlords array
3. Identify every landlord entry where location = 'Offshore'
4. Inspect sicCodeSicText1, sicCodeSicText2, sicCodeSicText3 and sicCodeSicText4 for each Offshore landlord
Expected Results:
1. Response returns HTTP 200 and valid JSON
2. At least one landlord with location = 'Offshore' is present in the landlords array
3. One or more Offshore landlords are identified in the array
4. All four SIC code fields are null for every landlord with location = 'Offshore'

Test Case Title: Landlord with a non-null SIC code has Onshore location
Preconditions:
1. DMS API is accessible with a valid function key
2. UPRN 100022917842 returns a response with at least one landlord
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /mees/property?uprn=100022917842 with a valid x-functions-key header
2. Parse the JSON response body and retrieve the landlords array
3. For each landlord in the array check whether any of sicCodeSicText1, sicCodeSicText2, sicCodeSicText3 or sicCodeSicText4 is non-null
4. For any landlord that has at least one non-null SIC code field assert that the landlord's location field equals 'Onshore'
Expected Results:
1. Response returns HTTP 200 and valid JSON
2. The landlords array is present in the response
3. Landlords are inspected for non-null SIC code values
4. No landlord with location other than 'Onshore' has any non-null SIC code value ? every landlord with at least one non-null SIC code has location = 'Onshore'

Test Case Title: SIC code fields are held independently per landlord in a multi-landlord response
Preconditions:
1. DMS API is accessible with a valid function key
2. UPRN 10010248290 returns a response with both Onshore and Offshore landlords (more than one landlord)
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /mees/property?uprn=10010248290 with a valid x-functions-key header
2. Parse the JSON response body and verify the landlords array contains more than one entry
3. Verify all landlords share the same uprn value ? uprn is a property identifier not a landlord identifier so this is expected
4. Verify each landlord has a distinct companyName confirming they represent different companies
Expected Results:
1. Response returns HTTP 200 and valid JSON
2. The landlords array contains more than one entry
3. All landlords share the same uprn ? this is correct as uprn identifies the property not the landlord
4. Each landlord has a distinct companyName confirming they are separate entities

Test Case Title: Support - Validation: Error messages are displayed on all support journey pages when required fields are skipped

Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Landing Page and sign in as an authenticated user; navigate to the Support Who Are You page via the 'Request Support' link; click Continue without selecting a role
2. Select role 'Department for Energy Security and Net Zero official'; proceed to the Support Contact Form page; click Continue without filling in any fields
3. Fill in the Contact Form (first name 'John'; last name 'Doe'; email 'john.doe@example.com'; confirm email); proceed to the Support What Do You Want page; click Continue without selecting an option
4. Select 'I have a question about the policy or guidance'; proceed to the Support Details page; click Submit without entering any details
Expected Results:
1. Error summary and field-level error message prompting the user to select a role option are visible on the Support Who Are You page
2. Error summary is visible and 'Enter a first name'; 'Enter a last name' and 'Enter a valid email address' field error messages are each visible on the Support Contact Form page
3. The missing option error message is visible on the Support What Do You Want page
4. Error summary and the details text area error message are visible on the Support Details page

Test Case Title: Penalty Calculator: Pound prefix in the rateable value input field is ignored when calculating the penalty
Preconditions:

Test Case Title: Property Details: 'Possible rental evidence from EPC register' shows 'Mandatory issue (Property to let) EPC transaction type' and 'Possible rental evidence from Companies House' shows 'Property owner has letting company Standard Industrial Classification code' when both evidence sources are present
Preconditions:
1. User is logged in and authenticated
2. User has navigated to the Property Details page for a property where the most recent EPC has transaction type 'Mandatory issue (Property to let)' (DMS possibleEvidenceEpcTransactionType = true) AND the property owner has a letting company SIC code (DMS possibleEvidenceSiccode = true)
Test Steps:
1. Select the 'Property details' tab
2. Inspect the 'Possible rental evidence from EPC register' field
3. Inspect the 'Possible rental evidence from Companies House' field
Expected Results:
1. The 'Property details' tab is displayed and active
2. 'Possible rental evidence from EPC register' displays 'Mandatory issue (Property to let) EPC transaction type'
3. 'Possible rental evidence from Companies House' displays 'Property owner has letting company Standard Industrial Classification code'
Comments: Bug 1031 - 'Invalid PossibleEvidenceEpcTransactionType for a property with multiple EPC Certificates'. Currently 'Possible rental evidence from EPC register' incorrectly displays 'Not found' instead of 'Mandatory issue (Property to let) EPC transaction type' for properties with multiple EPC certificates. Automation workaround asserts 'Not found' for the EPC register field. Revert workaround to assert 'Mandatory issue (Property to let) EPC transaction type' when Bug 1031 is resolved.

Test Case Title: Cookies Banner: Context and accessibility are verified on the cookies banner
Preconditions: 1. User is on the Landing page
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Landing page
2. Wait for the cookies banner to load
3. Run the accessibility scanner on the cookies banner
4. Verify all context locators are visible on the cookies banner
Expected Results:
1. Landing page is displayed
2. Cookies banner is displayed with 'Accept analytics cookies' button, 'Reject analytics cookies' button and 'View cookies' link visible
3. No critical accessibility violations are found
4. All context locators match the expected aria snapshot

Test Case Title: Cookies Settings Page: Context and accessibility are verified on the cookies settings page

6. Verify all context locators are visible on the Cookies Settings page
Expected Results:

5. No critical accessibility violations are found
6. All context locators match the expected aria snapshot
